script.coldOpen: [NO TALKING — 0:00-0:08] Show the official Minecraft Movie crowd scene playing on one side of a split screen. On the other side: your BBS Crowd Spawner spawning 100 custom NPCs in real time. Both filling frame simultaneously. Hold 5 seconds. Hard cut to black. White text appears: '$150,000,000.' Pause. Then: '$0.' Fade in your face.
script.hook: [0:08-0:25] 'So A Minecraft Movie just came out. B+ audience score. People losing their minds in theaters. And I watched the whole thing thinking — I can do this in Minecraft. Not the whole movie. Just the crowd scenes. The ones that cost them millions. Because I built something that does it in about three seconds. Let me show you.'
script.act1_problem: [0:25-1:00] 'Here's the thing nobody talks about with Minecraft movies or Minecraft cinematics — the world is empty. You build this insane city, right? Epic buildings, perfect lighting, the whole thing. And then you fly through it and it looks like a ghost town. There are no people. Minecraft just doesn't have crowds. And that's the whole reason the movie had to be live action — because making a fully animated crowd in Minecraft? Nobody had solved that. Until now.'
script.act2_tool_reveal: [1:00-1:45] 'Three weeks ago I built a Minecraft mod called BBS Crowd Spawner. It does one thing. You type a command, you pick any custom 3D model, and it spawns as many as you want, wherever you want, instantly. Watch.' [DEMO: Type /crowd spawn 100 15 [model_name] in chat. Show 100 entities appearing. Slow zoom out to reveal the full crowd. No talking — let the visual land.] 'A hundred custom NPCs. Three seconds. One command. And yes, they actually have textures — we fixed that bug yesterday.' [Small laugh — authenticity]
script.act3_scene_build: [1:45-3:30] 'So I built the scene. Same energy as the movie — crowd gathering, big open space, that chaotic feels-alive energy. Here's what that looks like with Replay Mod.' [CINEMATIC B-ROLL: 60 seconds of Replay Mod footage — slow pans, dramatic angles, crowd shots from above and ground level. Trending music underneath. No talking. Let it breathe.] 'That's 100 custom models. No Hollywood budget. No animation team. Just a mod I wrote myself and Minecraft.'
script.act4_comparison: [3:30-4:30] 'Now let me be real with you. The actual movie — it looks like this.' [Show 10-second clip of movie crowd scene or describe it clearly] 'Mine looks like this.' [Cut to your best Replay Mod shot] 'Is it the same? No. Is it insane that one person built a tool that gets it this close? I think yes. And here's what gets me — the techniques I used to build this mod are the same techniques the BBS mod community has been developing for years. McHorse built the foundation. I just extended it.'
script.act5_how_to: [4:30-5:30] 'If you want to do this yourself — here's exactly what you need. BBS mod 1.7.7 for Fabric 1.20.1. My Crowd Spawner mod which I am dropping in the description right now. Any custom 3D model you want to use — import it through the BBS model importer. Then it is literally one command.' [Show /crowd spawn syntax on screen] 'That's it. One mod. One command. Hundred NPCs.'
script.cta: [5:30-6:00] 'Minecraft Movie 2 starts filming in April. I will be building something bigger for that one — more models, better scenes, full cinematic sequence. If you want to see that when it drops, subscribe now. And comment below — what scene from the movie would you recreate? I'm taking requests for the sequel video.'
